package handler

import (
	"github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
	"gorm.io/gorm"
	"net/http"
)

// Positive scenario for gin_handler_returns_multiple_response_shapes
// Rule intent: Handlers that manually build several unrelated success payload shapes
// instead of delegating to clearer endpoints or render helpers.
func PositiveGinHandlerReturnsMultipleResponseShapes(c *gin.Context, db *gorm.DB) {
	viewType := c.Query("view")
	id := c.Param("id")

	switch viewType {
	case "summary":
		type SummaryResponse struct {
			ID    string `json:"id"`
			Title string `json:"title"`
			Count int    `json:"count"`
		}
		c.JSON(http.StatusOK, SummaryResponse{ID: id, Title: "Summary", Count: 42})

	case "detail":
		type DetailResponse struct {
			ID          string   `json:"id"`
			Name        string   `json:"name"`
			Description string   `json:"description"`
			Tags        []string `json:"tags"`
			Metadata    struct {
				CreatedAt string `json:"created_at"`
				UpdatedAt string `json:"updated_at"`
			} `json:"metadata"`
		}
		c.JSON(http.StatusOK, DetailResponse{
			ID: id, Name: "Detail", Description: "Full details",
			Tags: []string{"a", "b"},
		})

	case "export":
		c.JSON(http.StatusOK, gin.H{
			"format": "csv",
			"rows": []gin.H{
				{"id": id, "value": "row1"},
				{"id": id, "value": "row2"},
			},
		})

	default:
		c.JSON(http.StatusOK, gin.H{"id": id, "message": "default view"})
	}
}
